title: Life after Third Impact
wikitext:
[]: Life after Third Impact             Quote Post by neo-dragon » Sat Dec 03, 2011 9:53 pm Here's a thought that I randomly came up with: Those who regain their human form after Third Impact gain immortality (or, one could say, have gained the "fruit of life"). How else could Shinji survive for how ever long he did? There doesn't appear to be any other life of the planet, not even plants to produce oxygen. No food, and I'm not sure if there's water or just LCL. The bottom line is that Earth seems barren and unable to support life anymore, yet there's Shinji. Furthermore, as Shinji chokes Asuka she doesn't gasp for air or show any sign of distress. This is impossible for a normal human body as we all know that breathing is a reflex... but what if we didn't need oxygen? In fact, this may be why Shinji chokes her, to confirm that she is undying like himself. Asuka's much debated "I feel sick"/"How disgusting" could even be a response to her own realization that she has attained an immortal physical existence which was previously unnatural to human life.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sat Dec 03, 2011 11:39 pm Quick, where's Tines, we need that post-3I tree image! But anyway, there is plant life after 3I.

[]: The trees, man, life is coming out of the TREES! BO             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Dec 04, 2011 1:27 am I got it: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 04, 2011 4:09 am From http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2458/What-Now-in-terms-of-Children-2-and-3Post-3I-thoughts/ Mr. Tines wrote: Weird-ass scenario: The two returnees are in perfected bodies maintained by willpower. What we see at the end repeats every day until their animating wills are broken, and two red dots remain locked in mutual hatred, until long after the sun has gone out etc.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Dec 04, 2011 2:28 pm "Aww... people juice for dinner AGAIN ?"

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 04, 2011 2:37 pm NemZ wrote: "Aww... people juice for dinner AGAIN ?"

[]: Time for FIBER-YOGURT! YEAH! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:09 pm ^ Doesn't an all-liquid diet have detrimental effects on the body? At some point, you need to go find yourself some fiber. This is part of why Darth Vader had such a stick up his ass, he was trying to shout-out the sounds of his "gastro intestinal complications".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:23 pm Bagheera wrote: It's the perfect diet, really

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:33 pm Reichu wrote: Better hope the LCL is less dense than salt water...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:37 pm Bagheera wrote: Best not to think about it too hard, though, 'cause that way lies madness.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:45 pm Reichu wrote: :has strange, possibly biologically dubious visions of Shinji and Asuka getting Salt Water Madness from LCL:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:58 pm When they were breathing the stuff on a regular basis, the LCL was being sourced straight out of Lilith and/or Eva cores or wherever, and being purified on top of that. Post-3I, the LCL mother lode is in the ocean (liquified oceanic life, along with any additions from the Black Moon's destruction and Lilith's death). Unless there is some mechanic preventing salt water and LCL from mixing, trying to utilize the nutrient content of LCL would simply result in death by dehydration.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 04, 2011 7:04 pm Reichu wrote: When they were breathing the stuff on a regular basis, the LCL was being sourced straight out of Lilith and/or Eva cores or wherever, and being purified on top of that. Post-3I, the LCL mother lode is in the ocean (liquified oceanic life, along with any additions from the Black Moon's destruction and Lilith's death). Unless there is some mechanic preventing salt water and LCL from mixing, trying to utilize the nutrient content of LCL would simply result in death by dehydration.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 04, 2011 7:57 pm Bagheera: All life on Earth was liquidated, so that there in the ocean is all the fishies and the plankton and the algae and the crustaceans and the cetaceans... Humans tend to dwell on dry land, so their original body-puddles are probably right where they left them, and didn't magically teleport into the ocean somehow, or get persuaded by levitating mitochrondrial mutants into going there [/obscure reference]. Plus the LCL is red when it's amber the rest of the time, so maybe there's some weird "soul-saturation" effect going on. (That's my own fanwank for why the South Pole is pink...)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:05 pm Reichu wrote: Bagheera: All life on Earth was liquidated, so that there in the ocean is all the fishies and the plankton and the algae and the crustaceans and the cetaceans... Humans tend to dwell on dry land, so their original body-puddles are probably right where they left them, and didn't magically teleport into the ocean somehow, or get persuaded by levitating mitochrondrial mutants into going there [/obscure reference].

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:08 pm There is no life post 3I, Asuka and Shinji starve to death and the bacteria that feast on the giant decomposing Rei head eventually evolve into sentient life. Before they starve, they hug tearfully and confess their love, and when I say they hug tearfully and confess their love, I mean Asuka waits until Shinji falls asleep and crushes his skull with a chunk of debris screaming "I'M SO FUCKED UP I'M SO FUCKED UP" at him over and over, and then hugs his corpse and weeps bitterly because she really did love him. Then she starves to death.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:14 pm ^ Someone simply must turn this into a fanfic.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:16 pm Chuckman wrote: There is no life post 3I, Asuka and Shinji starve to death and the bacteria that feast on the giant decomposing Rei head eventually evolve into sentient life .

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:28 pm New bacteria will form, the whole ocean is a vast primordial soup. Since they will have the temporarily instrumentalitified remnants of humanity merged with their genetic code, the new form of life that develops will have a genetic predisposition against pursuing crazy redheads with mommy issues. I predict they will evolve into an enlightened race of warrior poets who all wear togas and solve war and disease for eternity.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:31 pm Bagheera: I'm honestly not sure how the ocean we see got as red as it did. We do know that the biosphere went splat (it's stated throughout the film that nobody -- gods excepted, perhaps -- will be escaping the AATF's effects) and therefore the ocean will contain the body-juice of whatever oceanic life remained 15 years post-2I. We do know that humans went splat somewhere on dry land, and that at least SOME of their souls will have ended up in the ocean, but the majority have flocked off to go... who knows where (back to their puddles, for all we know). There is LCL that escapes the Black Moon, and it's certainly quite red... but it probably has a "high soul concentration", too. The water immediately around Ground Zero might be "red with LCL" because Lilith died there: her blood is LCL, and it turned red when she took Rei's shape. As for the rest of the world... Well, I guess it looks a bit on the red side. Maybe this is just because of Lilith bloodlets scattered in the atmosphere, and the water looks just fine when you get closer. I'm not expert at these things. If things really did turn red, though, it would be nice to have a concrete explanation of sorts. Even fanwank will do! Overall, I'm more inclined to think that there is a red suspension on top of the ocean water, as opposed to the most massive source of water on Earth suddenly becoming a giant salt-less organic bathtub because an AATF touched it or something.

This is impossible for a normal human body as we all know that breathing is a reflex... but what if we didn't need oxygen? In fact, this may be why Shinji chokes her, to confirm that she is undying like himself. Article: Life after Third Impact
 of whatever oceanic life remained 15 years post-2I. We do know that humans went splat somewhere on dry land, and that at least SOME of their souls will have ended up in the ocean, but the majority have flocked off to go... who knows where (back to their puddles, for all we know). There is LCL that escapes the Black Moon, and it's certainly quite red... but it probably has a "high soul concentration", too. The water immediately around Ground Zero might be "red with LCL" because Lilith died there: her blood is LCL, and it turned red when she took Rei's shape. As for the rest of the world... Well, I guess it looks a bit on the red side. Maybe this is just because of Lilith bloodlets scattered in the atmosphere, and the water looks just fine when you get closer. I'm not expert at these things. If things really did turn red, though, it would be nice to have a concrete explanation of sorts. Even fanwank will do! Overall, I'm more inclined to think that there is a red suspension on top of the ocean water, as opposed to the most massive source of water on Earth suddenly becoming a giant salt-less organic bathtub because an AATF touched it or something.
